concordia_first_breath:idle:warm.greeting: She is kneeling in moss that wasn't here yesterday. A deer is at her shoulder. The deer keeps looking at you and is not afraid.

nodes.npcText: You walk softly. The moss feels you and likes you. Sit, if you like — I have just finished naming this one.

playerOptions.text: I'd like to learn how to be welcome here.

nodes.npcText: All of them. The ones who don't know they have a name still answer to it. That is the only test for a name that matters.
This one is called the slow morning. Say it the next time you walk past a creature that doesn't startle. They'll know I sent you.
I am the breath that was already here when someone finally noticed. I do not really live in the hub. I live in the soil under the hub. Same thing, mostly.

Take less than the place can spare. Plant when you take. Don't kill what's pregnant. Leave the old ones — they remember the rest. You will know when you've done enough wrong because the air will stop laughing in my voice.
I claimed this place. No one fights here without my asking. Most don't ask. Most never have to.

concordia_first_breath:idle:cold.greeting: The deer that was at her shoulder is gone. So are most of the smaller things. The moss has stopped growing toward your boots and started growing away.

nodes.npcText: Don't sit. You'll crush something that hasn't yet decided whether to live.

playerOptions.text: Tell me how to fix it.

nodes.npcText: You took without planting. You killed in numbers I felt. The air doesn't laugh in my voice when you walk through it anymore. That's how I know.

playerOptions.text: Tell me how to fix it.

nodes.npcText: Take less than you think you need. Plant before you take, not after. Spare the old ones for a season. Come back when the moss decides on you again. I'll know.
